transcript.clean_text: another hot day on le welter kind of getting used to these 32 celsius well the afterglow of victory for mass pedersen finally getting there in the green jersey in a race where we finished second on three previous occasions that felt good likewise evernote looking good with a margin of 2 minutes 41 over the field led by primus roglich sun was shining carnival atmosphere colorful as ever so indeed was the bike of j vine finally they added some polka dots to that and mountains would be the characteristic of the day albeit pushed right to the very end big tests in the offing as we run up to la pandera well with a flat start to the day this was going to beg a breakaway and there were so many incarnations of it it took a long time to settle finally we had 10 strong riders up the road with the likes of chapel zoun from ag to our citroen we had the senko again from astana luis leon sanchez made the break so did ameri from group armor conquer the italian from lotto sadal brenner was there as well but this man mads patterson in the green jersey he was hunting intermediate sprint points and was in the service of kenny ellison who is also up front for the mountains garcia from kern farmer and richard cara pass rounding out the group louisiana sanchez a huge danger and when it came to the penultimate peak of the day he was having some fun well it was not a game that richard carapaz was prepared to let sanchez play alone he bridged over to him and in so doing detonated the break meanwhile back on the mount they hit the ramps of 15 karapas looked fresh well the demands that had been placed on the team of jumbo visma by primus roglich were there to see they setting much of the pace since the breakaway thought about stage victory gaps were to be had and in the end there was to be some big ones roglic finally went for it out of the saddle ramco ever nepal unable to follow enric mass however he got a smell for it as well he came by and ever nepal looked to be struggling a train of talent coming up alongside him and pushing on including one a uso and of course rodriguez from india's grenadiers this looked like a disaster in the offing forever nepal like was for a user when he needed a bike change but he didn't panic took it on board and rode on so did cara pass pushing to the final peak the gap though was tumbling and roglic was on his case roglich in company enric mass with miguel anger lopez bridging over this was spectacular with the downhill sprinting run that would introduce us to the final ramps it looked like the gap may close but cara passed kicked on and even when it came down to 11 seconds we still believed so did ever nepal who was picking himself back up in the wake of this man who would head for a second stage win of this race but ever nepal had damaged limitation on his mind how bad could it be at the end we counted them in karapaz came home in his wake megalanga lopez picked up six bonus seconds four bonus seconds for primos roglich eight seconds down on the winner and then the clock began to count almeida came in great finish by him mass finishing with rodriguez shortly afterwards behind them aaron smand and then ever nepal finally appeared with the red jersey still in the lead of this race but by a narrowed margin coming home with a yuzo kelderman rounding out the top 10 on the day fabulous second win for richard cara paz great fortitude displayed in his wake not only by primus roglic who took advantage of some cracks in the armor of emma nepal this is how the day finished but overall is the crucial calculation evan nepal will be thinking they're coming for me he knows that and the queen's stage of course stage 15 still to be taken evan depaul now leads but by a reduced margin of 149 over primos roglich and rick mouse two minutes and 43 adrift this is going to be one heck of a battle by the time we head for sierra nevada ever nepal knows that how will he cope let's see what he's dealing with it's a very punishing run and we will be seeing it from flag to line what a day somebody will win somebody will lose but how big this race remains wide open you
